rationale: Both candidates converge on the same substantive theme echoed by dsocek's ground-truth comment (stop hardcoding component names, derive from an existing single source of truth), but Y backs this with deeper, independently-verified evidence — it enumerates _dit_modules across multiple pipelines and catches that the hardcoded list already misses MagiHuman's 'sr_dit', plus surfaces a second concrete, checkable finding (registry.py:427 vs manager.py:374 list divergence). X's parallel suggestion (use _dit_modules) is valid but thinner on grounding, and its bonus text-encoder-gap finding is more speculative. X does uniquely recall the xuechendi validation-request thread (before/after images) that Y never mentions, keeping it competitive on recall. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
